design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed=hash('Super vs Orchids|competitor_comparison|people comparing Super with Orchids|2026-07-06T10:34:14.272812+00:00') -> hero=comparison-lab, typography=Cabinet Grotesk, components=[gapless-bento-grid, decision-matrix, source-card-dossier], GSAP=[card-stacking, hover-physics]. AIDA Check: Nav->Hero(Attention)->Proof/Compare(Interest)->Motion/Media(Desire)->CTA(Action). Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk (Fontshare). Hero Math Verification: clamp headline fits 2–3 lines at 1120px max-width. Bento Density Verification: compare-grid uses grid-auto-flow:dense. GSAP Motion Selection: guarded card stacking + hover physics. Label Sweep & Button Check: black primary CTA, white secondary CTA, contrast verified.
headline: Super vs Orchids: personal AI agents compared for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Orchids. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost. Include a long-form buyer guide with a decision matrix, migration checklist, how-to evaluation plan, and source-backed caveats. Previous comparison-page failures omitted visible buyer-guide and decision-matrix language even when the article was useful; that must never happen. The visible page must include the exact phrases Buyer guide and Decision matrix.
site_brief.title: Super vs Orchids

summary: A fair, source-backed comparison for people evaluating Orchids alongside Super. We explain what Orchids is best for, where it fits in the agent market, and why Super is the sharper alternative for repeated computer-use workflows thanks to its reusable computer-use cache. Includes a Buyer guide, Decision matrix, migration checklist, and a hands-on evaluation plan.
